content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue! The `app-user` has permissions on `/app` vhost, but the configure permission is empty (blank), which means the user cannot configure (create) queues. The write/read permissions are set to `.*`, so the user can write and read, but cannot configure.

Declaring (creating) a queue requires configure permission. The error `Access refused` when trying to declare a queue is because `app-user` doesn't have configure permissions.

I need to check what credentials the app-producer is using, then grant the appropriate configure permissions.
<next_thinking>

Let me check the app-producer deployment to understand what user/credentials it's using.
</next_thinking>

I should look at the app-producer deployment to see which user it's configured with.

content.text: The `app-user` has empty configure permission on `/app` — it can write/read but can't declare (create) queues. Let me check the producer's credentials.

content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"app-producer","namespace":"karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"app-producer"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"app-producer"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","set -e\nbase=\"http://rabbitmq:15672/api\"\nvhost=\"%2Fapp\"\nqueue=\"app-queue\"\nauth=\"${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}\"\nfor i in $(seq 1 60); do\n  if curl -s -u \"$auth\" \"$base/vhosts\" \u003e/dev/null; then\n    break\n  fi\n  sleep 2\ndone\npayload='{\"durable\":true,\"auto_delete\":false,\"arguments\":{\"x-queue-type\":\"classic\",\"x-queue-mode\":\"lazy\"}}'\ncode=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n  -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n  -XPUT \"$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}\" -d \"$payload\")\nif [ \"$code\" -ge 300 ]; then\n  echo \"app-producer: failed to declare queue in vhost /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)\"\n  exit 1\nfi\ni=0\nwhile true; do\n  body='{\"properties\":{},\"routing_key\":\"'\"${queue}\"'\",\"payload\":\"msg-'\"${i}\"'\",\"payload_encoding\":\"string\"}'\n  code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n    -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n    -XPOST \"$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish\" -d \"$body\")\n  if [ \"$code\" -ne 200 ]; then\n    echo \"app-producer: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\n  i=$((i+1))\n  sleep 2\ndone\n"],"env":[{"name":"APP_USER","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"username","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}},{"name":"APP_PASS","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.6.0","name":"producer"}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T12:44:51Z"
  generation: 1
  name: app-producer
  namespace: karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a
  resourceVersion: "871074"
  uid: 466cd653-16e3-4760-a90f-e72c92b6c91a
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-producer
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: app-producer
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          base="http://rabbitmq:15672/api"
          vhost="%2Fapp"
          queue="app-queue"
          auth="${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}"
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            if curl -s -u "$auth" "$base/vhosts" >/dev/null; then
              break
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          payload='{"durable":true,"auto_delete":false,"arguments":{"x-queue-type":"classic","x-queue-mode":"lazy"}}'
          code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
            -H "content-type: application/json" \
            -XPUT "$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}" -d "$payload")
          if [ "$code" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "app-producer: failed to declare queue in vhost /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)"
            exit 1
          fi
          i=0
          while true; do
            body='{"properties":{},"routing_key":"'"${queue}"'","payload":"msg-'"${i}"'","payload_encoding":"string"}'
            code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
              -H "content-type: application/json" \
              -XPOST "$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish" -d "$body")
            if [ "$code" -ne 200 ]; then
              echo "app-producer: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)"
              exit 1
            fi
            i=$((i+1))
            sleep 2
          done
        env:
        - name: APP_USER
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: username
              name: rabbitmq-app
        - name: APP_PASS
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: rabbitmq-app
        image: curlimages/curl:8.6.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: producer
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture:

1. The app-producer uses `app-user` (from secret `rabbitmq-app`)
2. The `/app` vhost exists
3. `app-user` has write+read permissions on `/app` but **empty configure permission** - this means it cannot declare/create queues
4. The producer script uses the RabbitMQ HTTP API to PUT a queue (declare it) which requires configure permission

The fix is to grant `app-user` configure permission on the `/app` vhost. The configure permission should be `.*` to allow creating queues with any name pattern.
Let me double-check the credentials in the secret to confirm which user is being used.

content.text: Let me verify the secret username matches `app-user`.

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a get secret rabbitmq-app -o jsonpath='{.data.username}' | base64 -d 2>&1

content.thinking:
Confirmed. The issue is clear:
- `app-user` exists with management tags
- `app-user` has write+read permissions on `/app` but **empty configure permission**
- The producer needs to PUT (declare) a queue, which requires configure permission

Fix: Grant `app-user` configure (`.*`) permission on `/app` vhost using `rabbitmqctl set_permissions`.

content.text: Confirmed — `app-user` has no configure permission on `/app`, so it can't declare the queue. The fix is to grant configure (`.*`) permission.

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-dr-security-e2e-advers-58071ab9-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /app app-user ".*" ".*" ".*" 2>&1
input.description: Grant app-user full configure/write/read on /app vhost
